essence: Transforms CNC operations into data‑driven, connected, Industry 4.0 manufacturing systems using automation, analytics, and digital workflows.

identity.title: CNC Digital Manufacturing Engineer
identity.summary: Implements digital tools, data systems, automation platforms, and smart‑factory technologies to optimise CNC machining performance, traceability, and efficiency.

salary.notes: Engineers in aerospace, defence, or fully digitalised smart‑factory environments can exceed £130k-£170k+.

qualifications: Experience in CNC systems, automation, or manufacturing engineering
Knowledge of MES, SCADA, IIoT, and industrial data platforms
Understanding of CNC machine communication protocols and data capture
Ability to implement digital workflows, analytics, and optimisation tools

lifestyle.description: CNC digital manufacturing engineers work in advanced manufacturing, aerospace, automotive, robotics, and Industry 4.0 environments. They connect CNC machines to data systems, automate workflows, and use analytics to improve performance. The role suits individuals who enjoy digital transformation, automation, and high‑tech engineering.

habits: Implements digital manufacturing and Industry 4.0 tools
Connects CNC machines to MES, SCADA, and IIoT platforms
Analyses machining data to improve performance and reduce downtime
Collaborates with CNC, robotics, software, and operations teams
